content.text: Introduction to Pituitary Tumours and Surgery

Understanding your upcoming operation through a comprehensive endoscopic pituitary surgery guide helps ease anxiety and prepares you for every phase of your journey, from initial diagnosis to full recovery. When a growth develops within the master gland at the base of the brain, patients often feel overwhelmed by medical terminology and surgical uncertainties. However, modern neurosurgical innovations have transformed how these conditions are managed, offering highly effective, minimally invasive treatments that preserve your neurological and hormonal health. Prof. Giv Sharifi provides expert care across Dubai and Tehran, utilizing state-of-the-art technology to ensure optimal outcomes. Knowing what happens inside the operating room and during the healing period empowers you to take an active role in your healthcare decisions.

The pituitary gland plays a critical role in regulating vital body functions, including growth, metabolism, and stress response through hormone production. When benign tumours known as adenomas form here, they can cause hormonal imbalances or press against nearby optic nerves, leading to vision changes and persistent headaches. While medications can manage certain types of hormone-secreting growths, surgical removal remains the primary recommendation for tumours causing vision loss or hormone overproduction that resists medical therapy. Patients approaching this procedure benefit greatly from understanding the anatomical precision required by your neurosurgeon. Advanced imaging and sophisticated planning tools allow specialists to target the lesion with remarkable accuracy while protecting surrounding brain structures.
Understanding Transsphenoidal Surgery Techniques

Modern transsphenoidal surgery represents a major leap forward in neurosurgical intervention, allowing specialists to reach the pituitary gland without making external incisions on the head. During this procedure, the surgical team navigates through the nasal cavity and the sphenoid sinus, a natural air-filled corridor located right beneath the skull base. By utilizing high-definition endoscopes that project brightly illuminated, magnified images onto high-resolution monitors, your neurosurgeon can clearly visualize delicate structures. This sophisticated visualization technique enables the careful dissection and removal of the adenoma while minimizing disruption to healthy surrounding tissue. Patients experience significantly less postoperative discomfort and shorter hospital stays compared to traditional open cranial approaches.

The skull base serves as the structural floor of the cranial cavity, separating the brain from the nasal passages and sinuses. Operating in this intricate anatomical zone requires specialized training, steady hands, and profound knowledge of neurovascular relationships. Prof. Giv Sharifi employs advanced endonasal techniques to access deep-seated lesions safely, ensuring that critical nerves and blood vessels remain unharmed throughout the operation. Specialized instruments designed specifically for endoscopic corridors allow for precise maneuvers within confined spaces. This meticulous approach reduces the likelihood of complications and preserves normal pituitary function whenever possible, supporting a smoother overall trajectory toward restored health.
Navigating the Pituitary Recovery Process

Pituitary recovery begins immediately in the specialized postoperative recovery unit, where medical staff closely monitor your vital signs, neurological status, and fluid balance. Because the surgical corridor passes through the nasal passages, patients typically experience mild nasal congestion, pressure, or a sensation similar to a sinus infection for the first few weeks. Your surgical team will provide detailed instructions regarding nasal saline rinses and precautions to avoid blowing your nose, which protects the delicate tissue repair at the skull base. Most individuals spend a few days in the hospital before being discharged home, where healing continues with gradual increases in daily physical activity. Following all postoperative guidelines ensures your body heals correctly and minimizes the risk of infection or cerebrospinal fluid leaks.

Hormonal monitoring constitutes a vital component of the recovery phase, as the pituitary gland may require time to resume normal function after tumour removal. Some patients experience temporary diabetes insipidus, a condition affecting fluid regulation that your medical team can easily manage with appropriate medications. Long-term follow-up appointments with your endocrinologist and neurosurgeon allow for comprehensive hormone panel evaluations and serial magnetic resonance imaging scans. These routine checks confirm the complete removal of the adenoma and ensure that hormone replacement therapy is adjusted precisely to your body's needs. Maintaining open communication with your specialist team ensures any lingering symptoms receive prompt attention and expert management.
Preparing for Your Surgical Consultation

Preparing for your neurosurgical consultation involves gathering all relevant medical records, including previous imaging studies like magnetic resonance imaging scans and recent blood test results. Writing down a detailed list of your symptoms, medications, and specific questions helps make the most of your time with the specialist. During this appointment, your neurosurgeon will review your diagnostic findings, explain the proposed surgical plan, and discuss potential risks and benefits in detail. Understanding what to expect helps alleviate pre-surgical anxiety and fosters a collaborative relationship built on trust and clear communication. Patients seeking care in Dubai or Tehran can expect compassionate, personalized evaluations tailored to their unique medical circumstances.

Choosing an experienced neurosurgeon is one of the most important decisions you will make when facing pituitary pathology. Prof. Giv Sharifi brings extensive expertise in complex skull base procedures, combining advanced technical skills with a patient-centred philosophy. During your consultation, feel free to inquire about the surgeon's specific experience with endoscopic techniques and expected recovery timelines. Knowing that your health is in the hands of a dedicated specialist provides invaluable peace of mind as you prepare for your procedure. Your care team will guide you through every administrative and medical preparation step, ensuring you feel fully supported throughout your treatment journey.
Next Steps in Your Neurosurgical Journey

Embarking on the path toward surgical treatment requires reliable guidance and expert medical partnership. If you or a loved one has been diagnosed with a pituitary adenoma, taking proactive steps toward specialized care is essential for protecting your vision and endocrine health. Consulting with a qualified neurosurgeon allows you to explore personalized treatment options designed around your specific diagnostic profile. Whether you reside in Dubai, Tehran, or are traveling internationally for medical care, expert evaluation ensures you receive world-class attention. Reach out to schedule your comprehensive consultation and begin your journey toward lasting wellness today.
Frequently Asked Questions
What is endoscopic pituitary surgery?

Endoscopic pituitary surgery is a minimally invasive procedure where a neurosurgeon removes tumours through the nasal passages using a specialized lighted camera. This technique avoids external facial incisions, reduces recovery time, and protects delicate surrounding brain structures. Prof. Giv Sharifi utilizes advanced visualization tools to ensure safe and precise tumour resection for patients in Dubai and Tehran.
How long does recovery take after transsphenoidal surgery?

Recovery typically involves a brief hospital stay of two to three days followed by several weeks of resting at home. Most patients can resume light daily activities within a fortnight, although full internal healing takes a few months. Your surgical team will provide individualized timelines based on your specific procedure and overall health status.
Are there visible scars after skull base surgery?

Because the transsphenoidal approach utilizes natural corridors through the nose and sphenoid sinus, there are no external incisions or visible scars on the face or head. This cosmetic benefit is one of the primary advantages of modern endoscopic neurosurgery compared to traditional open cranial approaches. Patients can heal comfortably without outward reminders of their operation.
Will I need hormone replacement therapy after tumour removal?

The need for hormone replacement therapy depends on whether the tumour affected normal pituitary tissue and how the gland recovers postoperatively. Some patients regain normal hormonal function quickly, while others require temporary or permanent medication support managed by an endocrinologist. Your surgeon and hormone specialist will monitor your levels closely during follow-up visits.
How do I schedule a consultation with Prof. Giv Sharifi?

Scheduling a consultation is straightforward and can be initiated by contacting our clinic offices directly in Dubai or Tehran. Our administrative staff will assist you in gathering necessary medical imaging and records to ensure a productive first appointment. We are dedicated to providing compassionate, expert guidance at every stage of your neurological care.
